UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.goal: UAVs flying in the managed airspace will not collide with fixed ground-based obstacles (FGB)
UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.strategy: The safety of the UAVs can be determined by examining its navigation system, obstacle detection system, and control system.

subgoals.G1.1: The UAV's navigation system accurately identifies the location of FGBs.
subgoals.G1.2: The UAV's obstacle detection system can detect FGBs in its flight path.
subgoals.G1.3: The UAV's control system can effectively avoid FGBs.

solutions.Sn1.1: Navigation system test reports, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Obstacle detection system test reports, simulation results.
solutions.Sn1.3: Control system test scenarios, independent assessments.

Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.goal: Comply to infrastructure safety case (ISAC) requirements.
Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.strategy: Compliance to ISAC requirements can be achieved by meeting all the individual requirements outlined in the ISAC.

subgoals.G1.1: Comply to ISAC requirement 1.
subgoals.G1.2: Comply to ISAC requirement 2.
subgoals.G1.3: Comply to ISAC requirement 3.

solutions.Sn1.1: Documentation showing compliance to ISAC requirement 1.
solutions.Sn1.2: Documentation showing compliance to ISAC requirement 2.
solutions.Sn1.3: Documentation showing compliance to ISAC requirement 3.

UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.goal: UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based objects.
UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.strategy: The UAV's ability to avoid ground-based objects can be determined by examining its sensor system and navigation algorithm.

subgoals.G1.1: The UAV's sensor system can accurately detect ground-based objects.
subgoals.G1.2: The UAV's navigation algorithm can effectively avoid detected objects.

solutions.Sn1.2: Algorithm audits, simulation results.

Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.goal: Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) provides correct instructions for avoiding collisions between two UAVs.
Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.strategy: The correctness of the Collision Avoidance Algorithm can be determined by examining its ability to detect potential collisions and its ability to provide correct avoidance instructions.

subgoals.G1.1: The CAA can accurately detect potential collisions.
subgoals.G1.2: The CAA can provide correct avoidance instructions.

solutions.Sn1.2: Test scenarios, independent assessments.

GPS is accurate within 5 cms..strategy: The accuracy of GPS can be calculated by comparing the GPS reading with the actual location.

subgoals.G1.1: The GPS reading is within 5 cms of the actual location.

The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.goal: The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery is sufficiently mitigated.
The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.strategy: The hazard can be mitigated by ensuring the battery is regularly checked and the system is capable of alerting the user when the battery is low.

subgoals.G1.1: The battery check system is functioning correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The low battery alert system is functioning correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Battery check system test reports, maintenance records.
solutions.Sn1.2: Low battery alert system test reports, user feedback.

All identified causes for the hazard, El.goal: All identified causes for the hazard, El1, are sufficiently managed
All identified causes for the hazard, El.strategy: The management of the hazard can be determined by examining the identified causes and the measures taken to manage them.

subgoals.G1.1: Identified causes for the hazard, El1, have been accurately determined.
subgoals.G1.2: Appropriate measures have been taken to manage each identified cause.

solutions.Sn1.2: Risk management plans, implementation records.

The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.goal: The applied mitigations lead to sufficient risk reduction of the hazard El1
The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.strategy: The risk reduction can be determined by examining the effectiveness of the applied mitigations.

subgoals.G1.1: The applied mitigations are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The hazard El1 is sufficiently reduced.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessment reports, mitigation implementation records.
solutions.Sn1.2: Hazard analysis reports, safety assessments.

The mitigation requirement R1 holds.strategy: The mitigation requirement R1 can be satisfied by ensuring the system's resilience and the effectiveness of the mitigation measures.

subgoals.G1.1: The system is resilient to potential threats.
subgoals.G1.2: The mitigation measures are effective.

solutions.Sn1.1: System resilience test reports, third-party evaluations.
solutions.Sn1.2: Mitigation measures test results, independent assessments.

Contract-based formal verification shows.goal: Contract-based formal verification shows with sufficient confidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover
Contract-based formal verification shows.strategy: The verification of R1 can be achieved by examining the contract-based formal verification process, the design of the Inspection Rover, and the confidence level.

subgoals.G1.1: The contract-based formal verification process is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The design of the Inspection Rover is sound.
subgoals.G1.3: The confidence level is sufficient.

solutions.Sn1.2: Design blueprints, engineering assessments.

System remains functional for intended r.goal: System remains functional for intended radiation environment(NASA R&M mod) in order to complete science mission objective: Record the number of upsets in 28nm bulk SRAM in LEO for a period of 1 year.
System remains functional for intended r.strategy: The system's functionality in the intended radiation environment can be determined by examining its design, materials, and testing procedures.

subgoals.G1.1: The system is designed to withstand the radiation environment.
subgoals.G1.2: The materials used in the system are radiation-resistant.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has been tested and proven to function in the radiation environment.

solutions.Sn1.2: Material data sheets, radiation resistance test results.

System and its elements are designed to.goal: System and its elements are designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission (NASA R&M).
System and its elements are designed to.strategy: The system's ability to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses can be determined by examining its design, materials, and testing procedures.

subgoals.G1.1: The system is designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses.
subgoals.G1.2: The materials used in the system can withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has been tested to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses for the life of the mission.

solutions.Sn1.2: Material specifications, test reports.
solutions.Sn1.3: Test plans, test results, third-party evaluations.

System is tolerant to radiation-induced.goal: System is tolerant to radiation-induced faults and failures (NASA R&M mod).
System is tolerant to radiation-induced.strategy: The system's tolerance to radiation-induced faults and failures can be determined by examining its design, materials, and shielding.

subgoals.G1.1: The system design incorporates radiation-hardened components.
subgoals.G1.2: The materials used in the system are resistant to radiation damage.
subgoals.G1.3: The system is equipped with adequate radiation shielding.

solutions.Sn1.2: Material test reports, radiation exposure studies.
solutions.Sn1.3: Shielding design documents, radiation exposure test results.

System is able to recover from anomalies.goal: System is able to recover from anomalies affecting functions that are important to top-level expectations (NASA R&M).
System is able to recover from anomalies.strategy: The system's ability to recover from anomalies can be determined by examining its fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect anomalies.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can isolate anomalies.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can recover from anomalies.

solutions.Sn1.1: System logs, anomaly detection test results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Isolation test results, system design documents.
solutions.Sn1.3: Recovery test results, system resilience assessments.

COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.goal: COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requirement: No latch-up seen up to 5e9 protons/cm2 for 200 MeV protons.
COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.strategy: The COTS parts can pass the mission proton SEL requirement by meeting the proton energy and density thresholds.

subgoals.G1.1: The COTS parts can withstand up to 5e9 protons/cm2.
subgoals.G1.2: The COTS parts can withstand 200 MeV protons.

solutions.Sn1.2: Radiation testing reports, manufacturer specifications.

We see a world where everyone who choose.goal: We see a world where everyone who chooses to use these services, as well as those who do not, have confidence in a safe and secure sector...
We see a world where everyone who choose.strategy: The confidence in a safe and secure sector can be achieved by ensuring the safety of the services, the security of the services, and the accessibility of the services.

subgoals.G1.1: The services are safe to use.
subgoals.G1.2: The services are secure from external threats.
subgoals.G1.3: The services are accessible to everyone.

solutions.Sn1.2: Security assessments, penetration testing results.

There is justified confidence that cyber.goal: There is justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
There is justified confidence that cyber.strategy: The confidence in the cyber-security of UK regulated services can be justified by examining the security measures in place, the resilience of these services to cyber attacks, and the potential impact of any breaches.

subgoals.G1.1: The security measures in place are robust and effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The services are resilient to cyber attacks.
subgoals.G1.3: The potential impact of any breaches is minimal.

solutions.Sn1.2: Resilience testing reports, incident response plans.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be justified by examining the security measures in place, the resilience of the system, and the potential impact of any breaches.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO has robust cyber-security measures in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The system is resilient to potential cyber-security breaches.
subgoals.G1.3: The potential impact of any breaches is minimal.

Other stakeholders have justified confid.goal: Other stakeholders have justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
Other stakeholders have justified confid.strategy: Justified confidence can be achieved by demonstrating robust cyber-security measures, effective risk management, and resilience in the face of potential threats.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services at present
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be justified by examining the security measures in place, the resilience of the system, and the current threat landscape.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues will not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services in the future
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be justified by examining the current cyber-security measures, the resilience of the services, and the future plans for cyber-security.

subgoals.G1.1: Current cyber-security measures are effective.

ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.goal: ARO regulatory activity provides justified confidence in the security and reslience
ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.strategy: The justified confidence in the security and resilience can be determined by examining the regulatory processes and their effectiveness.

subgoals.G1.1: The regulatory processes are robust and comprehensive.

ARO has justified confidence in the over.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the overall regulatory institutions and standards

ARO has justified confidence that the re.goal: ARO has justified confidence that the regulated system can deal with cyber related events and changes

ARO has justified confidence in the tech.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the technical approaches for addressing cyber-security informed safety and resilience

ARO provides other stakeholders with jus.goal: ARO provides other stakeholders with justified confidence in the regulated security and resilience

Provides confidence existing system secu.goal: Provides confidence existing system secure and resilient

subgoals.G1.1: The system has robust security measures in place.

Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.goal: Sources of doubt actively managed - actual and perceived risks
Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.strategy: Managing sources of doubt involves identifying and addressing both actual and perceived risks.

subgoals.G1.1: Actual risks are identified and mitigated.
subgoals.G1.2: Perceived risks are identified and addressed.

solutions.Sn1.2: Surveys, communication strategies.

The vehicle is acceptably safe.strategy: The vehicle's safety can be determined by examining its structural integrity, safety features, and performance in safety tests.

subgoals.G1.1: The vehicle's structure is sound and durable.
subgoals.G1.2: The vehicle is equipped with necessary safety features.
subgoals.G1.3: The vehicle has performed well in safety tests.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements during product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The implementation of safety requirements can be evaluated by examining the design process, testing process, and compliance with safety standards.

subgoals.G1.1: The design process incorporates safety requirements.
subgoals.G1.2: The testing process verifies the implementation of safety requirements.
subgoals.G1.3: The product complies with relevant safety standards.

solutions.Sn1.2: Test plans, test results.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements post product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The implementation of safety requirements can be evaluated by examining the product's design, manufacturing process, and post-production testing.

subgoals.G1.1: The product's design incorporates all necessary safety features.
subgoals.G1.2: The manufacturing process adheres to safety standards.

The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.goal: The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Safety Requirements
The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.strategy: The vehicle's safety can be determined by examining its structural integrity, safety features, and performance in safety tests.

subgoals.G1.1: The vehicle's structure is robust and crash-resistant.

System is acceptably safe to operate in.goal: System is acceptably safe to operate in the specified environment
System is acceptably safe to operate in.strategy: The system's safety can be determined by examining its design, operation, and maintenance.

subgoals.G1.1: The system is designed to withstand the specified environment.
subgoals.G1.2: The system operates correctly in the specified environment.
subgoals.G1.3: The system is maintained regularly to ensure its safety.

solutions.Sn1.2: Operational logs, incident reports.

The residual risk associated with the id.goal: The residual risk associated with the identified system hazards has been reduced to an acceptable level
The residual risk associated with the id.strategy: The residual risk can be reduced by identifying and mitigating each system hazard.

subgoals.G1.1: All system hazards have been identified.
subgoals.G1.2: Appropriate mitigation measures have been implemented for each identified hazard.
subgoals.G1.3: The effectiveness of the mitigation measures has been verified.

solutions.Sn1.2: Mitigation plans, implementation records.

Functional Safety: The residual risk of.goal: Functional Safety: The residual risk of (hazard T) is acceptable
Functional Safety: The residual risk of.strategy: The residual risk can be determined by examining the risk reduction measures and their effectiveness.

subgoals.G1.1: Risk reduction measures are in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The risk reduction measures are effective.

solutions.Sn1.2: Risk assessment reports, incident logs.

Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.goal: Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goal U has reduced the occurrence or propagation of hazard T causes
Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.strategy: The effectiveness of Safety Goal U in reducing hazard T can be evaluated by examining the frequency of hazard T before and after the implementation of Safety Goal U.

subgoals.G1.1: Hazard T was frequently occurring before the implementation of Safety Goal U.
subgoals.G1.2: Hazard T is less frequent after the implementation of Safety Goal U.

solutions.Sn1.1: Incident reports, hazard logs prior to Safety Goal U implementation.
solutions.Sn1.2: Incident reports, hazard logs after Safety Goal U implementation.

Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.goal: Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by appeal to detection and diagnostics of systematic and random failures within the design
Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.strategy: The detection and diagnostics of systematic and random failures can be evaluated by examining the system's detection capabilities and diagnostic procedures.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect both systematic and random failures.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has effective diagnostic procedures for identified failures.

solutions.Sn1.1: System test reports, failure detection logs.
solutions.Sn1.2: Diagnostic procedure manuals, case studies of previous failures.

System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.goal: System Degradation: Argument by appeal over the transition to a safe system state in an appropriate time
System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.strategy: The system's ability to transition to a safe state can be determined by examining its degradation detection and response mechanisms.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect degradation in a timely manner.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can transition to a safe state within an appropriate time after degradation is detected.

solutions.Sn1.2: System design documents, test results showing transition times.

Random Failures: The impact of random fa.goal: Random Failures: The impact of random failures which can invalidate the Safety Goal U have been adequately managed
Random Failures: The impact of random fa.strategy: The impact of random failures can be managed by ensuring system robustness, implementing fail-safe mechanisms, and having a comprehensive recovery plan.

subgoals.G1.1: The system is robust against random failures.
subgoals.G1.2: Fail-safe mechanisms are in place.
subgoals.G1.3: A comprehensive recovery plan is in place.

solutions.Sn1.1: System stress test results, robustness analysis reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Design documents detailing fail-safe mechanisms, test results showing their effectiveness.
solutions.Sn1.3: Recovery plan documents, test results showing successful recovery from simulated failures.

A justified system safe state has been d.goal: A justified system safe state has been developed
A justified system safe state has been d.strategy: The system safe state can be justified by demonstrating that it minimizes risk and harm in the event of a system failure.

subgoals.G1.1: The system safe state minimizes risk.
subgoals.G1.2: The system safe state minimizes harm.

solutions.Sn1.2: Hazard analysis, safety testing results.

Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.goal: Restriction: Vehicle functionality has been restricted
Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.strategy: Vehicle functionality can be restricted in terms of speed, location, and operational hours.

subgoals.G1.1: Vehicle speed has been restricted.

The implemented pacemaker software is ac.goal: The implemented pacemaker software is acceptably safe to operate in VVI mode
The implemented pacemaker software is ac.strategy: The safety of the pacemaker software in VVI mode can be determined by examining its functionality, reliability, and performance.

The implementation satisfies all the des.goal: The implementation satisfies all the desired safety properties within acceptable timing tolerances

subgoals.G1.1: The implementation satisfies all the desired safety properties.

The model satisfies all the desired prop.goal: The model satisfies all the desired properties

The code generation process transforms t.goal: The code generation process transforms the model into the code correctly

The synthesized code satisfies all the d.goal: The synthesized code satisfies all the desired properties with the timing tolerance

subgoals.G1.1: The quality of information received by the traffic positions component is adequate.
subgoals.G1.2: The traffic positions component is capable of reporting failure when the quality of information is inadequate.

solutions.Sn1.2: System test reports, failure mode analysis.

Traffic positions component correctly im.goal: Traffic positions component correctly implements reasonableness checks and error detection protocol
Traffic positions component correctly im.strategy: The correct implementation of reasonableness checks and error detection protocol can be determined by examining the software code and testing the component under different scenarios.

subgoals.G1.1: The software code for reasonableness checks is correctly implemented.
subgoals.G1.2: The software code for error detection protocol is correctly implemented.
subgoals.G1.3: The component behaves as expected under different scenarios.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code review reports, software testing results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code review reports, software testing results.
